method: Sample 50 unique LinkedIn job IDs across 5 keyword variations (recruiter / staffing agency / sales representative / small business / consultant) × 2 SERP shards. Fetch detail pages and inspect description-text + descriptionHtml for emails, phones, external URLs.

results.anyEmail: 6/44 (14%) — overwhelmingly corporate boilerplate (EEO compliance, accommodations, HR support)
results.anyPhone: 3/44 (7%) — most are false positives matching the loose phone regex (e.g. "10389697" is a 8-digit number, not a phone)

exampleHits: reasonableaccommodations@airbnb.com (Airbnb accommodations team — boilerplate, present on every Airbnb posting)
EEOCompliance@spacex.com (SpaceX EEO compliance officer — boilerplate, identical across all SpaceX postings)
na.hrsupport@popmart.com (POP MART HR support — generic, not recruiter-specific)
structuredContact: LinkedIn's jobs-guest API exposes ZERO structured recruiter contact fields. The detail HTML at /jobs-guest/jobs/api/jobPosting/<id> contains only the company-level salary, location, employment type, applicant count, seniority — no recruiter / hiring-manager fields.
conclusion: contactEmail and contactPhone should be null for ~100% of LinkedIn jobs because the structured fields don't exist and description text-mining yields corporate boilerplate emails (not recruiter contact). Customers wanting first-found-email should read extractedEmails[0] explicitly.
actionTaken: Removed `?? emails.find()` and `?? phones[0]` fallbacks in src/transform.ts:mergeDetail. Marked contactEmail + contactPhone as platform-limitation in _tools/fleet-baseline-score.mjs.
